lines.x: The Year 937
Duke Erik and Professor Lierland D. of Piesberg Parliamentary University published this co-authored paper in The Journal of Urban History ('Where Did We Come From?' Issue 44).
The paper discussed the results of a detailed analysis of a collection of weathered remains.
The investigation into Sargon Excavation Site 4 revealed a common origin for Infected quarantines and executions with the law itself.
That is to say, the suffering of the Infected predates even the emergence of the law.
Lungmen, Support Structure Level, Exit
Now, we must fight.
If you can defeat me, it means some will be able to survive——
I am willing to join Rhodes Island. I am willing to fight together with you against the enemies of the Infected, your way.
This is my burden to bear.
8:50 A.M. - Four hours after the recapture of L. G. D. Headquarters - Near the Commandery Exit
L. G. D. and Rhodes Island Pharmaceuticals
Lungmen Search-and-Destroy Campaign - Final Phase
1. Control Reunion's movements within Lungmen. Complete under the direction of the L. G. D.
2. Mop up Reunion's forces still in Lungmen. Complete under the direction of the L. G. D.
3. Eliminate Reunion's operational commander, or their command influence. Complete under the direction of the L. G. D.
4. Eliminate the Reunion Movement before they can withdraw to the slums. Complete under a joint L. G. D. and Rhodes Island operation.
'...Attention all citizens... are performing an evacuation exercise...'
'...Those citizens who have failed to evacuate, please lock your doors, draw your curtains, and do not leave your homes...'
Does it have to be so loud?
Shoot that drone down already!
Now we're just caught up in an exercise? How dare they use such cheap tricks to cover up the exquisite terror I've worked so hard to formulate?
And to repeat it over and over again in both Lungmen and Ursine tongues!
I've had enough of these drones. Get! Hurry! Keep to the side streets!
Nothing from Scout Team 3 either!
Assault's signal suddenly c- cut out...
We're losing contact with all our overwatch teams one by one!
Why! How!
Such an insult... such humiliation foisted upon our movement!
But how, and why? Damn you, Lungmen! Damn the Lungmenites! Damn the uninfected!
(I've never seen him so unstable like this.)
(Faust, do we still have a chance to retreat?)
(I'll make some space for you.)
(If it's just your run-of-the-mill cops with the L. G. D. plus Rhodes Island, we should be able to get away.)
(What do you mean? Who else could be out there?)
Agh...! I can't believe this! I can't get through!!
If I can't even issue orders to my teams, how am I supposed to win?
(Someone is watching us. There is still more to this city.)
(Get ready. To survive.)
(Got it.)
(...Faust.)
(Hm?)
(We trust you.)
9:20 A.M. - Rhodes Island patrol route near the L. G. D.
They're really leaving this to the Infected? Rhodes Island got a raw deal this time.
And in Lungmen! Hmph. How could a city as big as Lungmen not have what it takes to fight off an invasion on its own?
Now they've got Infected fighting Infected on behalf of the normies...
Whatever. It's certainly not the first time.
Hurry up and clear a path! Don't look back, just break through their defenses!
If we can just get across this street, we have a shot at grouping up with the second brigade!
Don't give them a chance to return fire. There aren't many of us left——
And in a moment, there won't be any of you left.
What?!
Wh... who are you? Did you just drop out of the sky?!
Get back, get out of here! She... she might be able to fly!
...I really can't!
Identify yourself!
Use your scanner!
(Sorry, I have to deal with this first!)
Huh?
Lungmen law enforcement, listen up! I am from Rhodes Island.
Hold your fire. I really don't want to have to deal with crossfire while I'm fighting.
I can handle this on my own. You head on to the next roadblock.
Okay then. Back to you guys.
What's up with this chick?
I'll explain it to you. I'm sure you can figure it out. Probably.
(Deep breath)
I am Blaze, one of Rhodes Island's elite operators. My current objective is to cripple your combat capabilities.
The intentions with which you undertook this invasion of a foreign city-state notwithstanding, we aim chiefly to disarm you.
Setting aside the injuries and damages you have inflicted upon others, and attendant penalties, any attempt to resist at this point is pointless. Put down your weapons. You still have a chance.
Huh? What? You...
My fellow Infected, I give you a choice:
Surrender at once, and I will escort you to Rhodes Island, and subsequently deposit you somewhere welcoming to Infected.
She blocked it with her bare hands?!
Seeing as how you continue to resist, there's nothing more I can say.
As if we'd just let you lock us up? How crazy are you?
I'm not crazy! There's a time and a place for fight, and a time and a place for flight.
It's just that when you choose to fight, all I can do is fight back.
Either way, I don't need my chainsaw.
Sorry, I really don't want to fight my fellow Infected.
...it's so humid in Lungmen.
This stupid cat... is using Arts! Her body's steaming up!
My body? Guess again. Is it the air around me that's getting hot, or the air around you?
Let's take a Sami bath together. My treat.
Hah...
Luckily I did some warm up back in Chernobog. But really, I didn't even break a sweat handling you guys.
You! You Rhodes Islander, you're a disgrace to all Infected. How could you turn on your own like this just to help Lungmen?!
So you'll hand us over to them, as if that keeps your hands clean? Bullshit! How do you live with yourself?!
Huh?
You ate two punches, cracked your skull on the pavement and you've still got the energy to talk trash? Not bad.
But you've got it all wrong.
Mmf!
Having trouble breathing? Feel like your lungs are on fire?
Ack... Guh...
It starts in your sinuses, then crawls down your windpipe, until it finally finds a home there in your diaphragm. All I have to do is wiggle my little finger and...
A stream of superheated air will shoot into your body, expand, ignite your organs, and erupt out of your pores.
The funny thing is, Lungmen might let you live. But me and my little finger...?
Aah! Gaaah...!
Won't leave any of you breathing.
Nooooo! L-let me go! Please!!!
Fine. I'll be merciful.
Officer! Over here. You take care of them.
And one more thing, my new Ursus buddy. You think next time you could actually make me break a sweat?
Hey, they made it.
Dr. Doctor, over here!

opts: You're certainly making a lot of noise.

lines.x: Hah. That was nothing.
How about next time I show you how to snap an arm 90 degrees without breaking the bone?
Hey, what's with that face...?
How long have you been at Rhodes Island? You're acting like you've never seen a fight before.
Compared to them, I'm a regular softie.
But of course. You think I'm the type to play it low-key?
If I can put the fear of Blaze into them, who knows how many others might just give up.
I have to play it up to make all my hard work worth something.
Let's not steer the Doctor astray...
Bunny!
Don't just scoop me up like a toy!
But you're just so soft...! Hey! Stop struggling! Just let it happen. I promise I won't bite!
Blaze... Stop... Can't... Breathe!
Phew.
We've only been apart for an hour! What's with you! Get serious!
I take it back. You're not cute.
That's enough...!
Woah, hey, I was just playing! Get out of my head! I can't take you screwing around with my emotions again!
Wait.
She's here with you?
Hello, I'm...
I know who you are.
Talk to my fellow Infected like that again and I'll tear out your throat myself.
Blaze!
Sorry, Amiya.
But that's the way it is. And she deserves a warning.
I'm out. Rendezvous at the next waypoint.
GreyThroat, Blaze may have misunderstood you.
It's fine. I really did say those things.
She shouldn't be dredging up your past to attack the person you are today.
Who knows? Have I really changed? Even I don't know.

opts: You'll need to do something to find it out then.
Can we really have you running ops right now?

lines.x: I'm here because I wanted to do something. No matter what that Blaze says.
Sigh...
(Right... She's not Infected, so GreyThroat might not grasp the reality of Rhodes Island's current situation.)
(This operation could force her to decide once and for all... if she's going to stay or leave.)
(But she's gotten used to taking orders, and doesn't speak up much. Sometimes she does surprise, though...)
(There's no malice in her heart. She just needs a chance to find herself.)
(Still, she's strong and reliable. You can trust her, Doctor. And even if we can't help her, I know she can help you.)
Oh... where did Gavial go?

opts: Got too excited and went to crack some skulls?
Probably went to do frivolous collateral damage.

lines.x: She can't do that!
Wait a minute, who taught you to think of her like that, Doctor? I mean, it's kind of true, but still...
We can't afford to make mistakes here. Hurry to the next waypoint and try to signal her.
Even if she's operating according to plan, there'll be trouble if no one's watching her...
We've got elite operators acting as squad leaders. They'll report in. Don't worry.
We stand at a crucial point in this joint operation. This is the decisive battle for Lungmen.
She has her own mission to command.
Superintendent Ch'en, as Chief of the Special Inspection Unit, will be deploying the best the L. G. D. has to offer against Reunion in service of our joint plan.
At the same time, she has to herd Reunion's main force to the target location, all while keeping any of their smaller teams from slipping into the slums.
And we, Rhodes Island, will keep moving through the waypoints on our patrol route, mopping up Reunion's squads along the way, pushing them to give up the fight.
Finally, we'll reach the target location at the slums and, together with the L. G. D., we'll deliver the decisive blow to Reunion's main force.
Considering the scale, there's relatively little Rhodes Island can do. But we'll execute the plan to the best of our abilities.
As for the issues that have come up in the course of our cooperation with the L. G. D.... Dr. Doctor, I promise to answer all our people.
But, Doctor, there is something only you can do.

opts: Should I be taking this more seriously?

lines.x: Heh, good.
But it's not the kind of thing you'll have to put so much effort into.
Now's not the time to clam up!
Are you waiting for me to say more?
You could at least tell me what you're thinking, Doctor...!
No, that's okay!
This isn't the kind of thing you need to be so solemn about.
I'm going to call up Blaze and have her look after you. And for the little while until we meet up again, I need you to look after her too.

opts: Me look after her? Have you seen me?
I can protect an elite operator? News to me.

lines.x: Ugh... I'm not talking about like in battle! I mean look after her psychology.
You know Rhodes Island has to fight the Infected sometimes.
Especially right now. Our cooperation with Lungmen has led to some friction.
Blaze is one of the elite operators we rely on for survival, yes, but her psychological state has an impact on her performance.
It is because she is so resolutely committed to our mission that her encounters with the Infected have the potential to influence her emotions.
Go on, Doctor. Treat it as an opportunity to learn what makes her tick.
Show your appreciation for her. Thank her for saving you back there. She'll eat it up!
I believe in you, Doctor! Go get 'em!
Their main force is engaging our other teams!
We will reach the target location ahead of Reunion and join the formation!
Tell them we need to eliminate all Reunion elements that have not joined up with their main force first.
Right. Got it!
Do what Superintendent Ch'en just said! Over and out!
How long has it been since we fought together like this?
I wouldn't say we're 'fighting together' now. It's more like we're 'co-belligerents.' How did you get back here anyway?
I've got my own motor pool, of course. How else do you think I saved Rhodes Island? Now what's got your panties in a twist? I can tell you're upset.
Hm. Let me think.
You need to think about it! Look at you looking for reasons to be mad...
Oh, right. You went behind my back to save Rhodes Island. You could have told me in private.
Was that embarrassing for you? Or you just don't like being kept in the dark?
Both.
Wowee... since when are you so direct? My old Officer Ch'en would talk herself in circles for hours...
Eyes forward!
L. G. D. defender squad, shields up! Play it just like we have a million times before. And never forget, your brothers and sisters are right behind you! Stand firm!
Hold!
Crossbows, nock!
They blocked this road too? Uh oh... there's nowhere to go! We're trapped!
Relax, we can break them! With me!
Hold!
Guards, ready!
Overrun them!
——Now!
Crossbows, first volley, fire!
Pikes up!
Don't let a single one of them out of our city!
Are you on site?
Yes, that's right.
I know. But the L. G. D. has their job to do and you have yours. Don't forget your role in all this.
Indeed. Lin Kojui is quite opposed.
But this matter is beyond him!
I've heard all the opinions from all the stakeholders. But there is no wiggle room here. I do what must be done.
Kojui's daughter will reach out to you. In the next three weeks, you are not to contact me. Everything goes to her.
I leave it to you. Don't worry. I am watching you.
...There's no need for that. The past is the past. And it stays in the past.
Right. To the peaceful and stable future of Lungmen.
Wei, old boy, we're brothers, of a sort, aren't we...?
So I'm asking you, for the sake of my daughter and her mother...
Honestly, should I even have to ask? Would you really refuse to help your own sister?
I've already decided on a name for my daughter. We'll call her...
...Talulah.
Don't take it to heart. I understand.
It's all very clear to me.
Oh... Rosmontis, what are you doing?
I... I wanna fix this terminal.
Six years of memories up to last year... I saved them all in here...
This morning, I went to read the log from June 12, but the screen wouldn't turn on.
What if... what if I can't ever read them again...?
The March 14th log, when I went to the botanical gardens with daddy, or hiking in July, or the time when mommy...
...Mommy...
D- don't cry! Listen, you're not great with screens, right?
Why not wait until Scout can help you fix it? Or Closure?
I'll take it to Closure for you. She'll fix it up lickety-split!
Scout...
Is Scout that one operator who moves around all quiet?
That's him! You know him?
Yeah.
With that perfectly polished look... like a porcelain doll.
There he is.
Huh...?
I had yet to hear your estimation of me. I now understand how weak it is.
When did you get here?!
You could say I have always been here. I am used to concealing myself.
So Mr. Scout was here all along.
Why didn't Blaze sense it?
I... I wasn't paying attention. I just didn't notice!
Hey, if you were standing here all this time, why didn't you help her fix it?
Forgive me. We are mere moments from undertaking a mission. I must focus my energy on calibrating my weaponry.
You might ask Closure, or the good Mechanist to assist you.
Are you about to go save Dr. Doctor?
Yes.
Oh. You're going too.
It's me we're talking about. If I didn't go, what kind of joke would Rhodes Island's elite operators be?
And what did Ascalon say?
The usual. She's not going. Always there with Kal'tsit, that one.
Did it ever occur to you... That now is perhaps not the optimal time?
But it might be our last chance.
Chernobog is probably going to turn back deep into Ursus to avoid that Catastrophe. And a rescue operation in the Ursus interior is going to be damn near impossible.
I keep hearing you talk about it, but is this Dr. Doctor really worth all this?
Worth as much as Amiya and Kal'tsit.
Have I met the Doctor?
Not yet. But you will soon.
Good. I want to know what the Doctor is like too.
I really, really want to.
You will, kitten.
By the way, Ace, there's an uninfected girl named GreyThroat on your team.
I'm aware. She's been training in isolation.
Listen, Blaze. I've been working with her for a few months now. She has trouble expressing herself, but she's not a bad person.
She looked my teammate in the eye and asked, 'How hard could it be to be Infected?'
I know. But it might have been a legitimate question to her. She might really be curious.
It's not my place to demand an operator turn her opinion on a dime. And GreyThroat has never once questioned orders.
I don't care. I'm not going to stand by and let her insult my teammates, and I sure as hell won't let her disgrace the struggle of the Infected.
Now's not the time. I can see the rage you're holding back. But really, not now.
She said it right in front of me!
Take a breath, Big Cat.
You have a task at hand yourself.
...I do.
Fine. We'll deal with it when you get back.
Since when is Ace more senior than me?
When you take that tone, I start to think maybe you're screwing around.
She may very well be mocking you.
When he goes two days without shaving, he'll definitely look way more senior.
Ace's beard is very manly.
Hahaha! I have to go move some stuff around. Got my own job to do, you know. Later.
Let's get a drink when I'm done!
Try not to puke this time.
Misery spent two hours scrubbing the floor. I wager he would vomit himself when faced with that task again.
Who knew you could hold your liquor so well!
Rosmontis, Mechanist will give you back your terminal in a bit.
Be a good girl and wait patiently. But don't forget either!
I'll definitely remember! Bye bye!
Don't burn yourself!
Relax. I can control my own Arts.
...How is it you went before me?
Ace, was some uninfected girl like GreyThroat really worth training?
How much trust did we earn? And how much trust did we give?
If the Doctor...

opts: What are you saying over there?!
If I don't believe in Rhodes Island...?

lines.x: I'm saying bad things about you. To your face.
If there's a Doctor hanging around here, hurry up and come out already. You'll need some fancy Arts to hide behind that wall if you don't want me to find you.
Then I'll string you up on a lamppost as bait for Reunion. Don't worry! You won't die. A few bolts and a knife or two never hurt anyone.
....sigh.
Sigh!
Did Amiya send you?
Whatever. That's what she's best at. And what she's worst at...
I keep trying to remind her, to get her thinking about how she's still just a kid... it always seems to have the opposite effect.
I guess she'll never be able to put down that grown up persona of hers as long as she's the leader of Rhodes Island.
Of course, if it wasn't Amiya who sent you, if you decided to come keep me company all by yourself, I'm not against the idea.
...Hold me back? Haha! I could carry three of you on my back and I wouldn't even notice. One little Dr. Doctor can't hold me back.
Hey, look over there.
Go on! Get out of here! We don't want to fight!
Hmph... we won't yield to the Infected!
You're outnumbered! What's the point?
Duty!
Don't blame us for what happens! You see those monsters behind us? Just run already!
What monsters?!
See that Reunion group cutting through the alleys? They're picking at Lungmen's vanguard units. They won't hold for long...
Give me a path.
The fastest, of course!
Don't give me that look. It's not like I've never carried you before.
Here we go! Smash Reunion and save the Lungmenites!
Nnnng...
I'm sorry.
Now, me beating on you doesn't mean we aren't on the same side.
Haha! Yeah, no, seriously. It does. We're not on the same side.
This is Blaze. Come in, Team Blaze-4. Captured a dozen or so Reunion.
Come recover them when you can. Otherwise just hand them over to Lungmen.
Hey, Lungmenite, do me a favor and lower your crossbows. We're all friends here.
You still don't trust me? I saved your life.
Get back...! I mean, y- you're... you're Infected...!
Yeah. Yeah I am.
Ugh.
Come on, Doctor.
Move!
Sigh. All right, Dr. Doctor. Hop on. It's faster this way.
Hah. You've gotten used to my piggyback rides?
Don't look at me like that. How many times have we been treated like this? It's no big deal.
Let's head to Waang Saan Plaza in District 16. If Reunion wants to break through the line, that's where they'll have to do it.
If we're going to wipe out Reunion's main force, we can't let any of them slip through our zone of control.
The way Amiya and the cranky old cop lady worked it out, we'll just be whittling them away little by little until we can finally wipe them all out in one swift stroke.
So we go chase the bad guys out of Waang Saan Tower, herd them into a pincer attack, then meet up with Amiya and crush Reunion once and for all. Piece of cake, right?
...You and your faces. I told you I'm fine.
Fine. If you keep staring at me like that, I'm pulling this cat over.
Hold on tight.
Huh... Look over there.
Isn't that Mephisto's herd? But how. They need Mephisto around, don't they?
Wait a minute. They're... they're killing their own?
...What's going on?
I'm getting vitals, but no reports? And no sign of any distress signals either.
Something's not right...
Shouldn't you be, like, commanding? What are you standing here staring at?
None of your business, 'Little Miss.'
Hey, shouldn't we be sharing intel?
Not this time.
I'm thinking you lost contact with a spy.
Could you not waste your intuition on stuff like this?
At least you own up to it when I guess right. Infected?
Yep.
You think he turned?
Impossible.
You think the impossible happened?
If she turned... Well, I don't even want to think about it.
If she betrayed us, then there's absolutely no one left in Lungmen we can trust.
I think I know who it is.
Wait... Would she really be willing to do this?
Her? Really?!
Mephisto?
What was that you just unleashed? Your... team is more riled up than before.
They're... they're in pain.
Oh, you can tell? The seeds I planted have finally bloomed.
Nothing to worry about, Faust. They won't hurt us.
You're still catalyzing their infection?
Mephisto... just stop. We have a chance to survive.
I am the only one who can perform such Arts! And it is only through my Arts that we can escape!
You saw with your own eyes. If we push through like this, the L. G. D. will kill us all long before we can reach the escape tunnel in the slums!
Have you ever seen those weapons before? Nobody in Chernobog had weapons like that!
This is their city, they know it far better than us! Where did they come from? Where do they disappear to?! There's no way we can take them on with this few of us!
The Chernobog Ursus are too weak... Lungmen treats us like an army, but we're no army. We're just a group of like-minded individuals trying to survive!
Quiet down. Morale is low enough.
But my herd will never waver in the face of such challenges!
...not to mention all the Reunion members who just joined us.
FrostNova will definitely help us.
FrostNova? That FrostNova?
Hah! Hahah! As if she didn't take her Yetis and betray us?
Mephisto!
If not, we can definitely crush our enemies with such strength!
But even if they did turn against us, I can't blame them for doing what it takes to survive.
FrostNova is no traitor. I told you. Look at the facts.
Indeed... we all do what it takes to survive.
Just calm down, and don't go stumbling into the L. G. D.'s traps!
I have it under control, Faust. I will ensure the comfortable survival of all my people.
What...? What are you going to do?
I'm going to show the L. G. D. something much more terrifying than an army. Just wait until they see the flowers that bloom from my people.
Mephisto?!
The L. G. D. must pay for their atrocities... And that Ch'en... She will pay for what she did to you!
My comrades, my people, heed my words!
A magnificent vitality flourishes within you... Go forth and give in to your impulses, spread your seed, and seize your future!
No, Mephisto!
They're living beings!
(Chaotic howling and shrieking).
All of Lungmen is your garden! Go, now, spread the life within you to all the people! Go, go, go!
Huh? What?! What's happening?
Wh- what are you doing? You're not in our squad!
...What did I ever do to you?! Or Mephisto...?
Captain? Captain?! Captaaaaaain!!
Agh! A- are you... trying to... BITE ME?! Why a- aaaaaaaaagh!
Help! Somebody help me!!
Aaaaaaaaagh!
Heh heh...
They are far stronger than they were.
They can't hear my voice anymore, but they won't attack us again... They will grow stronger, more durable, and more numerous.
And they will completely consume Lungmen. Hah. If my people can't have this city, I certainly won't leave it to the Lungmenites!
Faust, come, it's time...
Faust?
What have you done?
(Pained wailing).
Faust? Where are you going?!
Faust!
Why are Mephisto's people attacking us?!
I saw it... It.. It was awful... Those things took Ivan... And... And then Ivan became like them!
Suka! What are you... some kind of monster?! They feel no pain. I stab them and they don't die!
They're getting stronger! We can't stop them!
Come with me!
Faust?!
No! It might be one of Mephisto's traps!
...He saved my life.
Mephisto's a piece of shit. He might be okay.
Get out of here. Hurry. The phantom crossbowmen will cover you.
Right.
Thanks... H- hey!
Look out!!
——Originium...?
Run! The herd's after you!
GreyThroat, the enemies movements are erratic.
The defenders will hold the line, while the casters cut down the enemy numbers. You and the snipers will take care of any Infected with Arts ability.
I'm allowed to shoot the Infected dead?
GreyThroat...
In principle, I can't allow anyone to harm the Infected.
Because, if I were to allow that, then wouldn't someone else allow the Infected to harm others? It's a vicious cycle.
But it's pointless. There's no room for the philosophy of ethics on the battlefield.
The ideal situation is that nobody hurts anybody. Regardless of their infection status.
Understood.
Gavial is providing medical support so... Watch yourself out there. Don't get hurt.
What's that...? It looks like... Mephisto's herd...?
But... I can't sense any emotion from them?
No!
GreyThroat, get down! Something's wrong!
They're... pulling Originium out of their bodies?
GreyThroat!
Doctor! Are you okay?
What's with this herd? They're acting completely different!
Doesn't matter. You're safe here. Kinda. Don't move!
I'll take care of them and be back in a sec!
How many men have fallen before you?
(Speaking Ursine).
Of course. I will bring it back for you.
...... (Speaking Ursine)......?
No. I'm not going to Chernobog.
Rest.
You'll be safe here.
Get some rest.
How many men have fallen before me?
Drop your weapon!
Just you...?
Move and I shoot!
Shoot first. Then ask questions.
What? He... disappeared?
No... I saw your file. You're Faust, one of their leaders. Your Arts won't fool me!
I know this is just an optical trick. If I shoot, you'll still have a bolt in your head.
Wrong.
Can you predict my movements?
The two of us, lone crossbowmen. If we both pull the trigger at the same time, we both die.
What's your name?
Codename GreyThroat. When did you get behind me?
The third time you blinked.
You're pretty direct...
You've been separated from your squad. I'm not here to kill people who don't want a fight.
We're enemies.
Rhodes Island is here to save the Infected. I saw you helping them.
Those were my comrades. You're my enemy.
When did Rhodes Island hire someone so naive?
Yes, I'm naive. All I can do is follow orders. I freeze up whenever I have to make a decision for myself.
You need to be careful.
Watch your finger!
That's a single shot crossbow. If I dodge your bolt, you're dead. Don't pull that trigger.
If you only follow orders, if you rely on everyone else to tell you what to do, that can only end poorly.
Why?
Most of the sheep who blindly take orders aren't actually dependent on anyone. They just haven't started thinking for themselves...
And those who have no direction in life, they're just empty machines looking for a sense of accomplishment, and meaning.
You activate, and perform your function, you input a goal, you achieve your goal, until you consume everything around you, and you consume those you serve.
I won't.
You're inexperienced.
I saw your interaction with the Infected back there. You're doing the right thing.
Your opinion means nothing to me.
Faust, you've been avoiding the L. G. D. Even the ones who are out here on their own, you haven't shot at them. I've been watching you.

Mephisto...
Mephisto. From now on, you are Mephisto. You are no longer what you were.
I don't trust you. I have no right to trust you. The only person you can trust is yourself.
What you did cannot be written off. You will always carry your past, even if you forget, even if you cannot understand what you did.
Everything you do, everything you go through, will become your kindling. They compel you, to keep the fire burning within you....
Until the whole earth is free, until you throw away all that others have given you, until you finally understand yourself.
At that point, you can even discard the name. It's your choice whether you stay or go, live or die.
Mephisto, I can't change your ideals. If you don't have it, go find it. We'll help ourselves.
Who am I? That's not important.
What should you call me?
......Talulah
I'm a rebel, an ordinary woman. I'm nobody. But for now, you can call me Talulah
And Talulah didn't say much to me.
She held me tight and rapped three times on my back.
Talulah trusted me. Yelena, Buldrokkas'tee, Lyudmila. They all believed in me.
I thought I could put the childhood I spent in that city behind me.
Only, things changed quickly. It started with what happened in that village.
It was a tragedy. But why did that tragedy make her this way?
Why did things turn out like this?
I'll do whatever you want me to do....
No need. Do as you please.
I allow it. You are Mephisto, after all.
But, you said before....
That was years ago.
It's only natural that some things change over the course of a career.
If we don't keep up with the times, we're doomed.
So we, and our fellow Infected, must fight for our collective future.
Anyone can be sacrificed for that ideal.
I can trust you, can't I?
Yes. Because I am Talulah.
Then I will!
You will, I'm sure you will.
We can't do it without you. You can do anything, and you can accomplish anything.
Come in.
You should have seen it all.
Who are you?
I am Talulah, of course.
The Talulah I knew... is not you.
Talulah is just a name. Talulah is just Talulah
Help him. Mephisto needs your help. Mephisto needs you to give him value. He trusts only you and me.
Talulah would never do that. You're taking advantage of his trust in you!
None of us have the power to choose.
You'll do it for him. Everything.
Or would you rather watch him die?
Don't you realize what happened? After passing through that village, Talulah changed completely! She's not the same person!
I know.
Then why are you doing this?
I don't trust anyone but you and her.
And I don't want to choose.
Faust, where are we going?
Faust, tell me. What should we do?
......We survive.
Isn't it painful to be alive?
It's painful.
But we can do it together.
This book, it's a book about ideals.
My ideals are....
GreyThroat! What's your position?
Amiya, I am providing support to an L. G. D. team.
We're about to engage!
Attention all elite operators: this may be our first, last, and only large-scale battle in Lungmen.
Everyone take objective one as your primary mission.
My team and I will be focusing on engaging the enemy commander.
I want you all to remember: top priority is completing the terms of our contract, that is to work with the L. G. D. to ensure the security of Lungmen.
But Rhodes Island's mission in this battle is to reduce the friction between the Infected and uninfected people, to prevent clashes!
We are Rhodes Island! No matter what else we carry into battle!
No matter when, no matter where!
And of course, if at any point our operations fall outside our standards, we will put a stop to them at once!
As such, we go into battle with a heavy burden of responsibility on our shoulders.
That was for my sake, huh?
She's such a worrywart...

opts: We'd better speed up, or we won't make it.

lines.x: You saw what tough customers those Infected were back there. That's a lot for one operator, or even one team to handle.
I really should've put that kid to bed once and for all back on the rooftop.
Oh, copy.
If you don't get your butt to the ambush point, I'll take all of last year's disciplinary reports and hand them over to Dr. Kal'tsit!
Settle down! I'll be there right on time!

opts: Could you put me down when you take a call?

lines.x: Blaze!
What am I supposed to do? We have to hurry...
(You'll pay for that one!)
Why so quiet, Doctor?
......Blaze!
I didn't do anything! It's the Doctor who clammed up!
Tell her already!
Come on, say something...!
Huh...?
Sorry, Doctor. I forgot you and Blaze would definitely go at it like this...
I do what I gotta do.
Why are you so pleased with yourself?
Anyway, all I need is your location and current operation.
You two seem to be getting along.
Sure are!
...Really?
(Wait, what's happening here? What should I tell her?)
(This is bad! Red alert! I'm taking psychic damage! I need guidance!)
Keep at it.
She hung up!
Oh no. We're in trouble.
But at least we're here. Let's take a load off.

opts: But this is the highest point in the district?

lines.x: Well... shit.
You know what they say, the shortest distance between two points is a straight line. Let's hop down.
Yep. Yawn.
Hold on... If I'm not mistaken... That's Mephisto's team over there to the east, moving to the ambush zone.
And over there... the scaly kid?
He's taking his team to fight something. What is all that black there?
Is that the herd...? With those numbers?!
Looks like we can't rest here, Doctor. Let's go. Time for the final battle!
Hey, Amiya. Hey!
I'm taking the Doctor into the fight!
What are you thinking?
We let a small Reunion group go, then we track them into the slums.
Weren't you leaving that to the special forces?
No reason we can't take the opportunity.
I've already looked into the situation once and one thing is clear: as long as we're there, the special forces can't act openly.
Once the fighting stops, they won't be able to do much.
You're not worried about orders from the top?
Who's going to stand up and take responsibility for the team?
All right, so you've got it all figured out. What's going on in the slums?
My informants have been busy. There's a few sectors I haven't been able to contact, but we have a lot more intelligence than anyone else.
This is a big gamble. But I guess we have no choice.
The L. G. D. is the only group that can face down the special forces.
Amiya, do you copy?
Officer Ch'en, we're in position!
...Mmhm.
I need to apologize for the whole ordeal with Reunion scooping up the Infected. That wouldn't have happened if we'd been more thoughtful.
...It's thanks to the L. G. D.'s quick action in the slums that Lungmen is able to plan against Reunion.
On a strategic level, I think you're fine, Officer Ch'en. But personally...
Officer Ch'en, Misha is dead.
And I can't condone your opinions of the Infected. Even you know...
The Lungmenites who love their city will fight for her, no matter who they are.
Wow, what an awesome little bunny.
Did I just hear Miss Swire?
Don't mind her.
Hey!
I agree with you, Amiya. Fighting for Lungmen can unite the people... except those tied up with Reunion.
But actually living in Lungmen is another matter. Time can't solve everything.
Of course. We have to solve everything ourselves.
Wishful thinking. The Infected and uninfected live in different worlds.
It's not wishful thinking, Officer Ch'en. It's thinking about Misha, and the countless other Infected who died just like her.
It was the gap between those two worlds that killed them. Are we going to let that divide define us? Are we not going to fight to bridge it?
I hope you can understand, Officer Ch'en, why it is that the Infected and uninfected of Rhodes Island come together to fight for Lungmen.
They're here, Officer Ch'en.
Prepare for battle.
It's an ambush! The L. G. D. is pushing us in!
Push through to the slums. Even though we haven't heard from Crownslayer... the slums are our only way out.
Take him.
What are you going to do?
Me and a few teams have encouraged the 'herd' to gather up here.
They won't attack me, so I can fight alongside them.
Fire in the hole!
We can't hold for long!
Ugh... Guhhh!!
It's those special Infected! Look out!
You'll bring up the rear?
You're not coming back.
Mmm! Mmhhh!!
Let him speak. He can't do his Arts like this.
What are you doing?!
Mephisto...
What are you doing?! You can't do this! Come back! Please! Come back here!
You once told me you would do anything I asked you.
Sasha!
You said you don't want to make decisions for yourself.
It's just, if you have your own ideas and your own goals now... I can't blame you for your choices.
If that's the way it is, Mephisto... Well, that's great.
But...
I'm out of options.
So let me tell you my last wish.
Sasha...? What are you talking about? I don't understand!
Don't let them escape! Take out their commanders!
...The Rhodes Island swallow?
(Mouthing 'pull the trigger.')
Hit the deck! Watch the crossfire! We've got them surrounded!
Fall back, Rhodes Island crossbowman! This corridor is ours!
Faust! Run!
No.
Crossbowmen, I know FrostNova is on her way. And even though Crownslayer is missing, her people are out there in the slums somewhere.
When FrostNova gets here, you're under her command.
I trust her. Once she arrives, you'll be okay.
And with my Originium Arts, you'll be invisible.
You can't, Faust! That will rupture your organs!
Doesn't matter. There's no reason not to do it anymore.
Retreat. That's an order.
And take him with you. But not to Chernobog.
...Faust...
You can't do this!
I hope you survive. Even on your own.
Don't die.
——That's my last wish.
Sasha! Sashaaaaa!!!
I don't want to hurt anyone. I don't want to get anyone hurt.
I wish we had just run away, back then.
We could have... just run away.
Sasha, you promised! We promised each other! We swore we would survive together!
It's too late... for me...
...I'm too tired.
Sashaaaaaa!!!
Goodbye, Eno.
He nocks a bolt into his crossbow, and winds it.
He takes aim, for the last time.
He stopped crying.
His eyes... it's like he's braindead.
I don't care about him. I miss Faust. He saved my life. He was a good commander.
Faust gave Mephisto to us... It was his last order. We're going to carry it out.
None of my business.
Get out of the city with us and you have a better shot at survival.
...Okay. Let's go.
Where are you taking him?
Faust said he doesn't want us to take him back to Chernobog.
...So where can he go?
How long were you with Faust?
Um... At first, I'd say he was with us.
Four or five years ago, Faust couldn't use any tactics and he really couldn't shoot a crossbow. We taught him all that.
He studied hard, trained harder, compared notes with the Yetis, sparred with Patriot's boys, and got stronger and stronger...
Patriot and FrostNova both taught him. He became our leader, even though we never much called him that, it's what he was.
You lucked out.
No matter how Mephisto got, Faust never made us do anything.
Archers, don't save your high-explosive bolts.
Won't those bolts be useful against the L. G. D.?
Faust insisted we honor the dead like this.
'It's not a waste to show respect.'
GreyThroat?
He...
If I recall correctly, he was the crossbowman with Mephisto, and one of Reunion's commanders.
...You knew him?
I don't know. I'm not sure if you'd say we... knew each other.

opts: It seems like there's not much Rhodes Island can do.
Have we ever actually saved any Infected?

lines.x: Yeah.
This is how it is for Rhodes Island... most of the time.
But even if there's only the tiniest bit we can do, we will do it.
We have to.
What are you thinking, Doctor?
Are you thinking about... all the death we've seen?
I can't guess. And I don't want to take it from your mind.
Because... I think one day you'll tell me yourself.
The operators have put in the work.
But death makes a heavy impression, heavier than saving someone. Every time we witness such death, it hits hard.
Just remember that without the efforts of our operators, we'd be seeing a lot more death.
So much more death...
I used to think Rhodes Island fought for the Infected.
But now I see all the dead at our feet, they're all Infected.
Amiya...
Why does Rhodes Island fight?
Now you ask?
Blaze.
...Answer her.
Honestly, I ask myself the same question all the time.
We see people, Infected and uninfected, die every day.
We don't, and we shouldn't fight to stop every death. That would be meaningless in the grand scheme of things.
Like now... like in this battle in front of us.
It wasn't one crossbow, one Art, one explosion, or one wound that causes all the death.
What we fight to defeat is the source of all of this... the hatred the uninfected have for the Infected, and vice versa, is the reason they kill each other.
We must tear up the soil that nurtures the hate. Using our pharmaceuticals, and our operations.
Ah——
Did Reunion fire that?
That was an Originium-boosted crossbow bolt.
Pretty lousy fireworks.
Nearl said, back in Chernobog, the scaly kid was pretty strong.
She told me her arms nearly broke. And I'll tell you from experience, I wouldn't be able to break them.
But back there, I knew his bowstring was as good as snapped, and he didn't have much Arts left in his weapon.
What happened to you, you little shit?
I think... maybe... he had no reason to fight anymore.
Hey, Amiya, would it be possible that he used some sort of Arts and survived from the attack?
...I'm sorry.
What are you sorry for? I just pity the kid. Looking at him, I can tell he wasn't a bad guy.
It's too late for any of that now.
No it's not.
The tragedies of the past carry on into the present... And we still have a chance to stop the tragedies of the future.
I'll get his crossbow.
Did she have an epiphany?
You don't know what she's been through?
She never shared. We're not close.
Her parents were Oripathy researchers, doing work for the Infected. They died in a clash between the Infected and police.
She just thinks her suffering and ours are the same.
GreyThroat is more sensitive than you give her credit for. She just doesn't express herself.
How am I supposed to know if she doesn't speak up?
Was he your friend?
...I don't know.
Who are you?
Should I introduce myself?
No thank you.
It's dangerous to make friends with the Infected.
I don't care if my friends are sick or not.
You have my condolences.
Why did you ask me that?
Because I worried you were making friends with Infected.
Call for you, Amiya.
This is Amiya.
Gavial? Where are you?
Under the slums? There's a passage? How?
...Black raincoats, and...
Yeti Squad? Are they forming up with Reunion in the slums?
...The worst is yet to come.
9:00 A.M. / Overcast
14 hours before the attack on L. G. D. Headquarters
'Bad Guy' - 300 Meters above 'District 14' of Chernobog
Mission objective: Join forces with Rhodes Island to rescue the reconnaissance team
Assigned assets:
'Bad Guy' and its pilot; Rhodes Island elite operator Blaze and her team.
14 individuals.
This is your stop. Amiya and her people are nearby!
Was it really okay to drop the team outside the city? You're gonna go down there by yourself?!
Come on, it's a lot better for them to be out there clearing a path and pushing back Reunion's pursuit forces than to fight here.
Okay. The terrain down there is pretty rough. This is as far as I can take you.
It's fine.
...At least let me put down a rope for you?
When have you ever seen me use a rope?
You're just gonna go for it again, huh?
Go for what? What are you talking about?
Every time we have a mission together, you go off and do crazy stuff while I sit in the cockpit on pins and needles and watch.
I'm kind of used to it, but that doesn't change the fact that you're way too reckless out there.
That just goes to show you what a good rapport you, me, and my team have!
Rapport my ass! Last time you demolished a town, bombed out a reservoir, and collapsed a mine!
Keep the chaos on the ground please, don't bring it onto my baby!
Fine, fine. I got it. But, 'Bad Guy,' really? Maybe think a little harder next time you pick a name.
Just jump already.
I think the welcoming committee's spotted us. This is where we say goodbye, Blaze.
No problem.
I'll wait for you by the entrance to the ruins. Be quick!
Huh? The comms.
HELLO?!
I can hear you! You don't have to yell!
Oh. Hi, Rhodes Islanders. I'm with the L. G. D. I've met up with your team by the ruins!
Didn't the L. G. D. withdraw already? What are you trying to pull?
I'm not like that Ch'en lady! Trust me, I can help you.
And how should we call you?
Use my codename. Call me Officer Swire!
Isn't Swire just your name?
Blaze, can you take care of this? I'm not good with bureaucrats...
......Blaze?
Don't look at me.
Get back here! Don't jump!
Didn't you tell me to jump a second ago? You can handle this. I believe in you! *Mwah*
Mwah my ass!
*Pant* *pant*...
...Is she okay?
Yes, it's nothing serious. Just her body temperature's a little low.
It's always... low. I'm fine. I just...
Frostleaf, slow down. Your temperature is even lower than usual.
Doctor, pass me a bandage.
I told you, I'm fine. Meteorite got hit a lot harder.
It's okay. I'm a Sarkaz. I'm more worried about you.
Jessica, what is Yeti Squad doing?
Th- they're approaching our position! About 500 meters out!
And the debris clearing?
We need about fifteen more minutes. The collapsed building has a pretty complicated superstructure and we can't risk blasting here.
Meteorite, get ready to fight. We have to hold this line.
Roger. Do we have a stock of bolts?
Yes. Logistics can get them for you...
Amiya, are you there?
...guh.
Did somebody leave their comms open?
This is the elite operators' channel, I can open it from my end.
That voice...?
Oh no. They sent her.
Um... who is it?

opts: Yes, who is this? And what's got her so excited?
Can she really make a difference here?

lines.x: They call her Blaze. She can certainly level the whole area and leave everyone pissing themselves.
Damn right it's me! But that last part is a little off.
Please... let's be serious here.
Where are you, Blaze? We need your support.
I'm right above you.
What?
Just about 200 meters to go and I'll be there!
I can't hit the machine, it's out of range!
Flying machines... they have such equipment?
Make sure they do not come down.
Wipe out the Rhodes Island squad before their reinforcements arrive. They cannot be allowed to join forces.
Wait.
———Stand back!
She jumped again?
She jumped again.
We can jump together sometime, Amiya! If you want.
I think I'll take a rain check. Where's your team?
I left them outside the city. They'll be here in a bit.
I can't hear anything over the wind. Turn off your comms!
Everyone... move to support Blaze.
Dr. Doctor, no matter what she says...
...just nod.
It dropped something...
...a person?
There's only one. But keep your guard up.
I sense a heat. She's using some kind of temperature Arts.
Hello, Reunion friends.
I don't like fighting other Infected. But I heard you hurt some of my Rhodes Island co-workers...
And you're still after my buddies. So I'm going to have to put a stop to that.
Aren't you mouthy...
Hi. How you doing, Miss Frosty?
I wanna see what happens when my hot meets your cold.
*Pant* *pant*...
Blaze!
I'm fine! They... have some moves.
You're not freezing, are you?
Thanks to you, we're okay. You're keeping the air warm enough to stop her chills.
Don't mention it.
Hey Frosty, why aren't you using lethal Arts?
*Hack* *cough*...
So that's why.
...FrostNova, she...
FrostNova's the name?
*Hack*... Help me... cover...
Sister...
Get out of here, Amiya. There could be more Reunion on the way. Do we have any injured? I can help.
So you're retreating?
I'm going to politely suggest that you don't fight anymore, snow bunny.
If you keep using your Arts like that, you're going to die. You're just burning your life into power.
Or, you could always come to Rhodes Island. We've got ways to extend your life.
Don't look at me like that... I don't need your pity.
Do I look like I pity you? You're strong. I respect you.
Why are you running away?
*Cough*... *Hack* *hack*...
Dr. Doctor, run. Don't look at her!
Reunion forces closing on our position!
You're not leaving.
Sister, don't use that now, not where you're standing!
We can't let them escape!
Who knows what Rhodes Island might do to our people in Lungmen!
The temperature difference... it's causing the structure... to weaken!
*Cough* *cough*... freeze!
*Hack*...!
It's working...
The Originium we buried has changed the structure of the lower levels, the plates are tilting!
Sister, it's too dangerous! Hold onto me!
Sister?!
...Blaze. Do you hear that?
Hm?
Is that the sound of the structure shattering?
Oh...
Buried Originium... heat absorption... temperature difference...
It's collapsing... FrostNova's Arts can actually do that...?
No!
The plate is warping!
Reunion is capable of something like this?
All I know is... the whole district is sinking!
Run! Get to a stable area! The ground here is caving in!
...Doctor?
Doctor, the ground under you... it's... splitting?
Uh oh.
Dr. Doctor, move!
Doctor!
Amiya, stay where you are! Dr. Doctor, grab onto that lamppost!
Save the Doctor!
The ground is splitting too fast! I can't reach!
Then I'll do it!
No!
What did——
Doctor!!
The buildings can't support their own weight anymore, they're all collapsing!
Meteorite, take her!
Okay, I've got Amiya!
Put me down!
Amiya, go. I'll get the Doctor! I'm an elite operator, I can do it!
Welp, looks like we're not quite done.
Doctor, I'm going to use airflow and pressure to slow your fall and push away the building beside you. Don't worry, it won't crush you!
Just don't panic! I've got you!
...See you soon!
Sup? Where's FrostNova?
Your commander fell down there too, huh?
...Yes.
Could we not fight?
Rabbit of Rhodes Island, could we forge a temporary armistice? Honestly, we never much wanted to fight you.
But...
Mephisto told our sister you'd killed Alex, and she was very concerned about what you would do next.
...Who's Alex?
Skullshatterer.
I'm sorry. I did kill Skullshatterer.
Infected really should not fight one another. I'm sorry.
What's to apologize for? It was a battle, wasn't it?
It was.
So let's have a ceasefire. Nesti, what do you say?
Huh? *Ursine profanity*!
Nesti says he's fine with it.
I don't think that's what he said.
As long as we're not in battle, there's no reason to kill anyone, right?
Why is it... you want a ceasefire?
Did you ever notice that both our sides are led by rabbits?
...Huh?
I think something in my brain just broke.
Hm?
These are the yetis of the ice field?
Wh- what do you mean?
Shouldn't they be cold, merciless killers?
This isn't right.
That's prejudiced.
Listen here, bunny.
Who wins the battle, or where you go, none of that matters to us. All we care about is our sister's life.
And you're worried about your friend too, right?
...Right. We didn't come here to hurt anyone.
You're definitely not here to hunt Reunion for sport, are you?
Of course not!
You know... it's funny...
If it weren't for Patriot insisting on unity in the Reunion Movement, we'd have dealt with Mephisto and his people a long time ago.
And that'd be kinda like hunting Reunion for sport, huh?
Huh...
Let's get digging, shall we?
Move out.
But we're really not familiar with the layout here. I'm worried... what if the Infected resist our operations?
Relax. I know the area. Just follow my orders.
Yes, Sir!
Sir, bad news: intelligence reports Reunion is moving with some degree of organization again. It seems they've found another commander.
And... and they've reencrypted their communication channels. We can't intercept them anymore!
Could they have joined up with the units in the slums?
Impossible. Rhodes Island should already have dealt with the situation there. There's no risk of us getting boxed in.
Strange...
Ah Ch'en, I'm ready to——
A transmission?
'Little Miss,' would you mind giving me the room?
How do you work it out so you're busy every time I have something for you?
Is that spy of yours back on the grid?
I said give me the room. And get me some finballs while you're out.
I'm not a kid, you know.
...This is Ch'en.
We have another Reunion element up from the lower levels of the slums.
Infected called Yeti Squad. It seems they have the ability to collapse buildings and destroy infrastructure.
Tread carefully.
...Understood.
Only-1, Lungmen's special forces are operating in the slums. We are at the moment incapable of stopping them. Exercise caution.
Roger.
...Only-1, why did you drop out of contact?
I had some things on my mind.
It's clear to me now. I'm leaving Lungmen to you.
What? I don't understand.
Me, now? I don't belong here.
Only-1!
I'm going to take some of Lungmen's Infected. The L. G. D. can't protect them. The city doesn't care about them.
In other words, this city isn't at all what you think it is.
Nine! You're under orders!
I haven't been with the L. G. D. for over three years.
And actually it was me giving orders to you.
Nine...!
Those special forces you're talking about? I think I've seen them around.
I thought too much of this city. Goodbye, Ch'en.
Oh, right. Don't go snooping around the sewers.
Was I too loud?
You're pretty loud. I think the whole block heard you.
And yet I'm still not as loud as you.
Hey, Ah Ch'en, get over here.
Have a seat.
Wow, Miss Swire squatting on a dirty street corner. You're not afraid to soil your clothes?
Certainly not! What makes you think I'd worry about a thing like that?
I was just about to give that stinky sewer rat another call.
I may not come here often, but I'm pretty sure those special forces are up to no good.
Have you met Lin? From the slums?
I have. He's a nice old man. Nicer than my grandpa anyway.
They call him the 'Rat King.'
The men behind the curtain aren't all like such. But he's really a good person.
I never thought Yühsia would turn out like this. She was such a shy and timid girl, the kind who would always hide behind you.
I don't think she's changed. She just... took on responsibility. Maybe the kind she couldn't handle.
It wouldn't do any good to tell her that. We're between a rock and hard place here.
I can't say I understand what's going on between her and her father, but I know she's not so cold-blooded.
So I'm gonna call her.
That report I just got may be of use to us.
What do you mean?
A Reunion group with special powers has entered the slums, and they're able to command other Reunion elements.
And I'm the only one who knows. Well, now it's the two of us, I guess.
What are you thinking?
We steer the raincoats into them.
Aside from the group left to help the L. G. D. clean up the new-type Infected, there's also quite a few of those special forces with us in the slums.
And the situation is: we don't know their distribution, we have no way to guard against them, and have no idea what they might do.
But with Lin Yühsia's help, we can guide them into that Reunion team.
Force them out? Show them to the whole L. G. D.?
The L. G. D., sure, but also Rhodes Island.
You trust them so much?
They chase their objectives harder than any group I've ever seen. They're even more dedicated than the L. G. D.
And who else are we going to trust?
You've got stones, I'll give you that.
Ordinary Lungmenites don't know Lin, they don't know your grandfather, they don't even really know Wei Yenwu, let alone any chambers of commerce or whatever behind the scenes.
They just trust the L. G. D. None of those players would dare disband the L. G. D., and leave us with the regular old police.
Then you add in the outsiders... And there's no way they'll want to take over.
They'll fire you though.
Whoop-de-doo.
Then let's wrap up this one last job! Next time, their patrol route goes right into the slums.
You can get Lin on board?
As long as we look after this place, Lin will work with us.
One more thing...
What's that?
Did you know we have three guests?
What is it?
Listen up, stinky rat, I've got news for you. There's a Reunion unit we can't hold. We need your team's help.
Impossible. What are you trying to pull?
Have I ever lied to you?
You said we'd go to the same secondary school, only for you to run off to Victoria.
I'm sorry. Really, truly sorry.
Forget it. I don't care.
Yühsia, your dad has done so much for Lungmen. We don't want to lose this place, and the people here can't lose us either.
Liar. You're coming here?
You'll come to the slums?
......Ch'en will.
You really are a terrible liar. What business have I with her?
For whatever reason, your dad wasn't able to resolve the conflict between the Infected and the normal people, but Ch'en can.
Ch'en and your father will rebuild the slums together.
They're working to turn it into a place where all kinds of people can live, to the point that we won't even be able to call it the slums anymore.
Think about it... Even though most Lungmenites don't live here, they've been coming here a lot in the past few years, haven't they?
Soon, we won't need any slums.
What?
We haven't been able to solve the Infected crisis, but I know... We can make this a better place.
They'll be Lungmen's downtown Districts 22 to 29. No longer 'slums.'
This city was built on the many, many different kinds of people all coming together to make it what it is today.
Lungmen can't afford to lose anyone.
Yawn. How boring. You've become as boring as Ch'en.
Yühsia...
Look, I'm not trying to make a soap opera here.
Where is that Reunion unit?
Oh?
I won't ask again.
Amiya, have you entered the slums?
We have a bit of a situation here. Let me fill you in.
*Pant* *pant*... Impressive!
The black raincoats are retreating! Run while you can!
Thanks!
Don't thank us. If you have the strength, you should fight alongside us!
Did you see him swing down from the fourth floor and graze my head like that? If I didn't freeze his weapon with the Originium FrostNova left in my backpack, I wouldn't be standing here!
Very impressive. They're just like the swordsmen in the gas masks back in Ursus. Even stronger, really!
You almost died, what are you so excited about? The masked swordsmen you're talking about were the Ursus Royal Guard! We only met five of them that time!
Did you count how many raincoats there are?
Does that mean we're even more awesome than before?
This is not a time for jokes. Their techniques and their equipment mean they must have a city... Even the craftsmen of multiple cities supplying them.
There's no way we can beat them back on our own. Don't even think about trying to kill them!
I just... I just think I did pretty well.
The point is this situation is way beyond what we can handle... We can't hold out for long!
Quiet!
Reporting in.
Sister, these are... Faust's people.
*Cough* *cough*... I recognize them.
FrostNova, we received your message, and...
Why is Mephisto with you? What happened to him?
Mephisto has taken some... hits to his psyche.
Him? Traumatized?
Wait, where is Faust?
Faust stayed behind to give us, and others, a chance to escape. We left him outside the slums.
It's hard to come back from that.
Faust was a brave warrior.
FrostNova, if we could, we would like to fight alongside the Yetis.
You could. We must get our remaining people out safely.
Find a sniping position and await orders. We will need your support in the coming battle. We don't know when the enemy may attack again.
Understood!
Pass our location to all teams: We will guarantee a safe route for their retreat from here!
Keep evacuating.
Understood!
Are they like the Ursus soldiers with a total disregard for collateral damage?
I don't think so. The Ursus were willing to use direct artillery fire on the upper city!
I knew they were brutal. But that brutal?
We saw it with our own eyes.
The raincoat people are very different.
They see civilians as targets, since they can't tell the difference between Infected and uninfected. They don't know if maybe we're dressing up as civilians.
The Ursus just didn't care, but these people don't let a single one slip through.
There are so many fleeing, it's damn near impossible to keep order! FrostNova is in a tough spot!
Sister won't let anyone die. Reunion or no.
We came to Lungmen to fight. How did we end up saving people? It's funny.
The fighting is pretty much done. If you guys didn't show up, we'd all be dead!
Man that's crazy. You're really counting on us?
Can you not talk like that...? I used to think you guys were so imposing, but now you just seem like a bunch of clowns.
You got us all wrong. We just don't talk much. Shouldn't be imposing.
Anyway, aren't we supposed to be holding this place with you?
You're totally right! Bro, let's defend this place together!
What's going on?
We... we found some locals locked up in here.
A mother and daughter. We were going to use the place as a firing position, but we don't know how to communicate with them... They seem pretty scared of us.
(Local language)!
(Local language) ...... (local language) ......!
She is your child, yes?
Waaahhh!
Such a healthy one.
Don't be afraid. Don't cry. Your mama is right here.
Mama is here.
Waaahhh...
Mm... Um... Mm.
Hehe.
Oh, the child is smiling...
Get her out of here.
...... (Local language)?
You *cough* escort her. See her to safety.
They're uninfected. There are so many of our people who need evacuating. We don't have time.
Oh?
...Right. Who cares whether it's the Infected or the uninfected now? Understood.
Erm, uh, ma'am, um... Komm zis way?
Neidei, neidei... hou.
FrostNova, Sister, wait for us to come back. Don't try to hold on your own.
Have some faith in your big sister.
Hurry! They're coming!
They are deploying some units wearing other uniforms... They might be Lungmen's L. G. D.!
*Cough* *cough* ... *hack*.
Go!
The temperature is as low as it will go!
The uniformed ones are no match for us, but watch out for the raincoats!
Where's Nesti? We need more firepower. We need suppressing fire!
He says okay!
I don't think Sister's body can take much more. She can't keep the temperature down!
We can't break the pavement?
No way! If we do that, how will our people and the locals get to the exit?
We're sure this is the only way out?
Yes. The other ones have all been destroyed. All that's left is the one we dug.
How much time do we have? Or how much time does Sister have?
Why must it come down to tormenting her so?
They're almost done!
I'll go get Sister!
Sister, we've just about got this group evacuated!
And the fighting... how goes?
We've got the L. G. D. completely suppressed. The terrain favors us, and they can't make use of their superior numbers.
But...
You have nothing to hide.
Lungmen's raincoat brigade doesn't care about the terrain, or the people. They... they're going to level everything.
They're purging the slums
And those uninfected Lungmenites we saw...?
I'm afraid that was their doing.
Sister, stop your Arts! We can handle these ones ourselves. Just rest for a bit!
*Cough* *cough*...
Sister!
The temperature just cracked the lower limit!
She's doing it...! The superstructure is weakening!
Smash their footing and push them back! Fire!
They're falling back! Keep it up!
Faster! More! Faster!
R- right!
That's our big sister! She really did it!
Petrova, what's wrong? Why do you look so gloomy?
...Sister?
Young, come here.
No.
Come!
I can't!
You have to. Sister doesn't have much time left.
Waaahhh...
I'm hungry!
Where's daddy? Where did he go?
I want my daddy...! Daddy! Daddyyy!
Daddy will come get me!
A child?
Blaze-4, Amiya-1, come in. I'm in the slums. 4th Street, building number 10. There's a child survivor, Infected.
Waaahhh...
...Hello.
Wh- who are you?
Are you a Lungmenite?
I will not hurt you. I don't speak Ursine well. I am not an enemy.
What did you say?
Come with me. It is dangerous here.
I can't... Uncle said to stay here.
But I will take you to find your dad.
You can do that?
I can do that.
You, hiding behind me, come out.
You're...
You're not with them.
You're... from Rhodes Island?
Child, stay back. Don't come closer.
Drop your weapon and come out with your hands up.
What?
You're not here to save us?
Don't you Rhodes Islanders know what's hidden here?
We worked hard to set up this ambush... If we can't lure them in, if we can't take them out, they'll kill all of us!
You're using children as bait. You deserve to be killed.
You think this will end when we're dead?
Even if they kill us, they won't let the children go. They won't let anyone go!
If that's all it took, we'd gladly die to save the kids!
We didn't even attack you!
Who's killing children...? The people in the black raincoats?
You've seen them, you understand! They're slaughtering everyone!
That's no excuse. After I take you in, I will apply to have you protected.
And we're supposed to just trust you? That crossbow of yours is a killing tool, what are you protecting?
For now, I'm protecting your lives.
Save it. We'll deal with you, then reset our trap!
We've already got the order to form up and retreat. If we die here, it would dishonor those comrades who died to save their children!
...Child, take cover!
Hm...
I'm still alive?
You think you'd be better off dead?
Operator Blaze?
It's not like you dying would solve anything. Better keep on ticking.
What about... the Reunion men? And the children?
Operator GreyThroat, I got you all wrong. And I'm sorry.
The children!
I took care of the Reunion guys! I hid the kids! Just listen to me already!
No need... as long as the children are safe.
Can you not be obnoxious for like five minutes?
What did you get wrong about me?
I watched you stand up to fight for Infected kids. It was hella leng noi.
Your Lungmen dialect sucks.
Hey, that's off the top of my head! But really, I thought you were just some heartless uninfected girl, joining Rhodes Island to spy on us or whatever. I got you all wrong.
If I were a spy, this is how I'd get you to trust me. As long as you weren't stupid enough to let me bleed out...
You bandaged me?
I'm still an elite operator. It's my job.
Anyway, you're right.
But even if you were a spy, you still did the right thing. So I'll save this spy's life and then pack her off to Legal.
I could just run.
Let's not be stupid here. How are you gonna outrun me?
Trust doesn't mean letting your guard down. Trust means knowing what you'll do and what you won't.
If you do the right thing, I'll back you up. If you do the wrong thing, I'll correct it, or stop it, if I have to. That's trust.
Thanks, GreyThroat. You trusted me, and you corrected my wrong thoughts with your right actions.
And I'm sorry.
But you really need to work on your people skills. They didn't want to be difficult, that was a show for survival.
If you screw up, you still need to go to Legal.
Whatever.
I think so... at least Blaze is trying. Leave it to her to drop her prejudices so quickly.
Rhodes Island's elite operators have more than just their incredible combat skills. Blaze is also gifted with a gregarious personality and savoir faire. As well as her own opinions.
If you want to lead her, if you want her support, you'll have to gain her trust. And first, you have to learn how.
I've fought alongside Blaze for a long time. And I think... I think you can too, Doctor.
But okay... isn't good enough, is it?
She puts up a carefree front, but Blaze has a complicated personality.
Give it time, keep your eyes open, and I know you can do it, Dr. Doctor.
But you need to put in more work!
Learning to trust each other isn't easy, but it needs to be done.
We need your power, Doctor, and you need to win over the operators to turn those tactics of yours into reality.
That's a lengthy process, but it's one you need to experience.
Blaze is an elite operator who excels in assault operations. She doesn't have much to offer in the way of strategic thinking.
She really needs your support. Or you could think of it like she'll turn your strategies into reality on the battlefield. She's a professional.
I'd say she's already cooperating.
Like, look where we are now...
Our operators follow our orders out of a sense of duty, not trust.
Real trust is built on understanding. The kind Blaze is working on with GreyThroat.
Oh...
Looks like we've got company. More Reunion.
Let's give Blaze a little more room to apologize. She still needs to stick her foot in her mouth a few more times.

opts: Then let's buy them some time!

lines.x: Yep.
She's a good conversationalist, but not so great at expressing her feelings.
What's really going on here is her confronting her deep-seated prejudices more than just an apology to GreyThroat.
Normal folks' prejudices against the Infected aren't a one way street... we have our own issues with the uninfected.
The enemy within is just as much an enemy as the enemy without.
...We will.
Listen up.
Those Reunion guys were scared out of their wits.
Neither we nor the L. G. D. can put that kind of fear into someone. They were ready to go against everything they stood for.
They met something else... But what was it out there that could do that to them?
And... will we have to fight them too?
(*Pant, pant*...! *Pant, pant*...)
(...A trap?! Was that attack back there just a feint?)
(If she keeps cutting up my team, the whole unit will be worthless...)
(I have to contact them right away!
(Grrr, why can't I move my fingers? Why am I shivering?!)
(I... I can't let her get me!)
Nggah!
(Low growling).
Projekt Red, don't kill her.
*Hack* *hack*...! Gah!
Is it her?
Yes, definitely. It's her scent.
*Sniff sniff*
Ick. Fake. She is no wolf.
But the scent is real.
The scent of a true wolf. The first true wolf.
True wolf?
You... filthy cub!
Don't move.
Mmf, how can I...? My muscles won't even respond.
Can... can you control me?
She tried to use a wolf technique, a bite she learned from wolves.
She wants to fight back. I can sever her spine, and paralyze her.
No need for that.
Ok.
Wait, you...
I know you... I finally found you...!
Traitor... you traitor! I found the traitor!
And you are...?
Ilia's daughter, Lyudmila?
Shut up! You have no right to speak my father's name!
Ursus destroyed all the lab, and all the scientists.
Is that what you think?
How dare you?!
It was you! You're the disgusting traitor who sold out my father with Sergei, who sold out all the scientists!
Your hair is the same color as his.
...What are you talking about? Why would you...?
Don't move.
Agh!
You're wondering why I have no reaction to your accusations?
Because you're scum!
Scum. And what type of scum should I be?
Should I be a coward, out for wealth and luxury...? Or should I be cruel and calculating, doing whatever it takes to achieve my goals?
The me, standing here before you, does it fit your image?
I never thought... I never thought you were a heartless, cold-blooded woman!
Did Sergei give you a heartfelt confession before you killed him?
...What are you...?
Did you know he was Alex and Misha's father?
Hah... Hahaha...! Alex helped me find him!
...Reunion took in Alex, killed his father, and hunted down his sister.
Hah...
Projekt Red, let her move.
Ok.
...What do you want?
To give you a chance to kill me.
A chance? Do you know what you're saying?
You're giving up on life? You want to repent, like Sergei, who sat on top of Chernobog after selling out his colleagues?
...Oh you'll regret it. You'll regret everything you ever did!
'No regrets,' I always say...
But actually, there is one massive regret I will share with you today.
——But I'm afraid it has nothing to do with your father.
I'll kill you. I'm going to kill you!
*Hiss* Haah...
'Swallow the bitterness of the dead'...
Fog?
It's Arts.
No, not fog... This is smoke.
The nervous system stimulates a smoke-emitting organ in the oral cavity. It is indeed an Originium Art that relies on this Infected organ.
You stand there talking your mouth off... You're beyond saving. Are you so scared you can't even show it?
Why did you betray my father? Why did you betray the scientists?
Why?! Tell me why——
'Director Kal'tsit'?!
Lyudmila, I only barely made it out alive myself.
You expect me to believe your bullshit?
Hah. Your father read you 'Truth and Justice,' didn't he?
To give a five-year-old a book like that... Ilia really had no sense.
But he loved that book. One summer day, he carried you, and the book, past the window, the bushes orange against the sun. He made you wave to me. You pouted and turned your head.
Enough!
That night, he asked me: what if we just sealed up the sarcophagus and never told the Ursus about the machines inside? Would we be able to save more people?
Would we be able to prevent the Ursus from using these machines in battle?
Are you saying all of this to... to confuse me?!
Ilia was one of the scientists who came up with the plan to seal the sarcophagus. And his convictions influenced his entire life.
You have his fighting spirit. But there was more to him than that...
Lyudmila, you call me a traitor, you want to kill me. You could do that, but——
When you choose violence, you are doomed to one day meet something stronger than you.
Hah hah...
Could you be more arrogant? No one, no caster can penetrate my fog. You have no idea where I am.
You're going to die screaming. I need you to die screaming.
...... (Sticking out her tongue).
For the scientists, for the infected, I have to kill you. You Ursus puppet! You piece of trash!
Do I look like a caster to you? I am Infected though, you got that right...
Come, Mon3tr.
...Huh? What... what's that?
H- how? Your spine?!
I guess you can see through the fog, huh?
How...?
Ack...! Wh- what are you...?!
What is that?!!!
I am not a caster. I've never relied on Originium Arts.
Go, Mon3tr.
(Happy hissing).
Nnng... Gaaaagh!
What is it?! What is that thing?!
Aaaaaah! Don't touch me! Let me go! Let go of meeee!
Why can't I pierce its shell? Aiee! Don't touch me! Get away!
Annihilate...
Aaaaaahh!!
...the falling object above her.
(Dissatisfied hissing).
You're too excited. The building is going to collapse. Don't let her get crushed.
Mmmg......
(Low humming).
Just kill me.
What?
...You can obviously kill me easily! Don't tell me you're the type who plays with your prey!
If I can't kill you, what do I have to live for?
Just remember, even if I'm dead, I've still cursed you forever!
...Are you truly prepared to die?
Do you want to die?
If I am your traitor, will you feel better after killing me?
...No more... talking! I'll kill you! I will!
Ilia was my student, and my friend.
Your father was one of my finest researchers. His dedication and selflessness could have pushed Ursus at least five years into the future.
I don't want to hear anymore! Why do you insist on telling me this stuff?!
You do want to hear it. Because you've always doubted yourself.
You never let Sergei explain himself. So I'll do it for him.
——I don't betray people. Not even Sergei.
He was indecisive, but not greedy.
The Ursus authorities had his newborn son and daughter. He gave up your father's location to save their lives.
And now, Alex and Misha are dead. He wasn't able to save anyone, in the end.
If... If I had gone to save Misha, maybe she and Skullshatterer... would still be alive.
N- no! No way! Look how powerful you are! Why didn't you save my father?!
(Threatening growling).
Because I couldn't.
I couldn't have convinced your father to leave the institute.
Lyudmila, I sent you to Siracusa before I left Chernobog. And yet you went back.
Oh, you're so like your father. Sometimes, willpower trumps time itself.
You would always return to Chernobog, just like you would meet me here.
So this is all meaningless? Everything my father did, everything I did... was for nothing?
No. The researchers died to protect our secret.
Ursus to this day has no way to analyze the contents of the sarcophagus. They've given up, and sealed off the installation.
Now, there's just a little energy left to harness, powering the core of Chernobog.
If I betrayed anyone, it's the Empire of Ursus.
Your father's efforts meant something. Even if Ursus has long since covered it all up, you can remember.
I will remember.
(Yawn).
...But all that you did? What was that for?
Ursus purged the entire institute and slaughtered every scrap of compassion in any of their scientists. The real traitor was the Empire itself.
Look at you. You've killed Sergei, you've killed a man who couldn't protect his own children.
Now you're in Lungmen. If you and your Infected friends really manage to take over the city, what will you do to the uninfected?
Massacre them all? Enslave them? Exile them to the wilderness and let them die of exposure?
Or cut them all with Originium, turn them into Infected, spread your pain to them?
...Or will you peacefully coexist with them?
Could you do that, Lyudmila? Could Reunion, as it is now, do that? Is that how you want to build your Infected nation? Or is that how you want to fight Ursus?
If the latter, why come to Lungmen? If you seek to terrify your enemies, what can you do to be more terrifying than Ursus?
(Kal'tsit, she is confused. She is scared. She wants to run.)
Mon3tr, return.
Is that okay?
She can hear me.
'Crownslayer,' your hate as well as that of your movement has been used and abused, toyed with.
Think of your father. Remember what Ilia taught you.
Think, on whom do you want revenge?
The one leading you down this path of terror and assassination... Your leader is not a sane person. Your leader has no heart.
A leader true to her ideals would not let someone else die for them, much less let someone commit murder on their behalf.
Run. Leave this place. Take a different path.
Take a path that can truly change this world... If that's what you want.
Her scent is gone.
Did you always want to see her? Why not kill her?
Should we kill a homeless hyena, even if the home isn't real?
A hard question. I don't know.
Projekt Red, do you think killing me would make her happy?
No. She is in pain.
We are not the law. We cannot judge people. We have the power to kill others, but no power to claim it is right to do so.
If she took pleasure in the act of murder, I would have killed her on the spot. Without question.
I know. She is not happy. Her breathing is erratic.
She has now become a virus. A unique strain spreading 'unease' among the Infected fanatics of Chernobog.
...How many years does a person live? And how many paths can they take? We spend more time blind than we do seeing the truth.
But then, seeing the truth and making a choice are two different things.
When she awakens from this dream of collective revenge, she will leave Reunion. But even so, Sergei's blood, and that of the people of Chernobog, will always be on her hands.
Projekt Red... When you make a choice, there is no going back.
I understand. I will do my best. I will only kill wolves.
Good girl.
Do you remember that scent?
I remember.
What is it like?
...Not bad.
It's a bloody smell, but not like you were just saying, with the heart and stuff.
How silly. Talulah is a Draco, how could she be a wolf?
Hmm.
Time to go?
Yes, let's go.
...The temperature's dropping. We're running out of time. Let's step up our efficiency.
All of the Reunion teams I could reach have pulled out.
That's a lot of people.
It's incredible... like a river.
It'll be a river out there in the wilderness... a river of refugees.
There's Ursus and Lugmenites out there. Infected and ordinary folk alike. No matter what they came with, they're leaving empty-handed.
They thought there was a place that would take them. Now they know there's never anywhere they could've gone.
This river won't flow forever.
(He sounds so sad.)
(He picked that up from Big Daddy.)
(No wonder. Anyone would get sentimental at a time like this.)
If Big Daddy was here, we would've won the fight and it wouldn't have come to this, huh?
It's the luck of the draw. No use thinking about how things could've been.
